name: node-corepack
title: node-corepack
summary: Access npm, Yarn and pnpm without having to install them
description:
Access npm, Yarn and pnpm without having to install them
Corepack is a zero-runtime-dependency Node.js script that acts as
a bridge between Node.js projects and the package managers they are
intended to be used with during development. In practical terms,
Corepack lets you use Yarn, npm, and pnpm without having to install them.
.
Node.js is an event-based server-side JavaScript engine.

commands.name: corepack
commands.description: Access npm, Yarn and pnpm without having to install them

arguments.description: Remove the Corepack shims from the install directory

arguments.description: Store package managers in a tarball
arguments.raw: --output  Store package managers in a tarball

arguments.description: Store package managers in a tarball
arguments.raw: -o  Store package managers in a tarball

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use corepack because Access npm, Yarn and pnpm without having to install them.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use corepack as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `Corepack - 0.24.0` as documented upstream for corepack.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `corepack <command>` as documented upstream for corepack.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `corepack <binary name>[@<version>] [... args]` as documented upstream for corepack.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `corepack disable [--install-directory #0] ...` as documented upstream for corepack.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair corepack with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `corepack --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
